TextENGNew: Naturally, the cunning detectives didn't interrogate us together.
changeReason: Different characterization: 'smart enough' vs 'cunning'; Japanese 狡猾な means 'cunning/crafty', not just 'smart'

TextENGNew: Ooishi didn't come to my room.
changeReason: Different: 'come to talk to me' vs 'come to my room'; Japanese 私の方に来なかった means 'didn't come to my side/room'

TextENGNew: His target had always been Satoshi-kun.
changeReason: Different: 'nobody else' vs 'had always been'; Japanese 最初から means 'from the beginning/always', not 'nobody else'

TextENGNew: I got stuck with a young detective who just made small talk.
It was probably a technique -- ease into the hard questions through casual conversation.
changeReason: Different: 'trying to ease me into the interrogation' vs 'a technique -- ease into the hard questions through casual conversation'; new is more specific about the method per Japanese 話術 (conversational technique)

TextENGNew: ...Knowing that, his attempt to match my wavelength felt like a third-rate host club act. Nauseating.
changeReason: Significantly different: 'the clumsy way he spoke began to frustrate me' vs 'his attempt to match my wavelength felt like a third-rate host club act. Nauseating'; Japanese ホストクラブ (host club) comparison is present in Japanese but missing from original

TextENGNew: Was Satoshi-kun holding up?
Ooishi surely considered my alibi testimony a desperate bluff.
changeReason: Different: 'didn't think Ooishi really bought my testimony' vs 'Ooishi surely considered my alibi testimony a desperate bluff'; Japanese 付け焼き刃 means 'makeshift/desperate measure', more specific

TextENGNew: He must have been convinced that one hard push would break Satoshi-kun.
changeReason: Different: 'trying to get Satoshi-kun to confess' vs 'convinced that one hard push would break Satoshi-kun'; Japanese 一気に畳みかければ落せる means 'could break him with one push', different characterization of method

TextENGNew: ...All I could do was pray that Satoshi-kun would keep silent, no matter how hard they pressed.
changeReason: New translation adds 'no matter how hard they pressed' (どんなに辛くても = no matter how hard), present in Japanese but absent from original

TextENGNew: The internal phone on the desk rang.@k@|@yThe detective picked it up.
changeReason: New translation adds 'internal' (内線 = internal/intercom), a detail from the Japanese

TextENGNew: He hung up and told me someone had come to pick me up.
It seemed only I was being released.
changeReason: Significantly different: 'Satoshi-kun had to stay' vs 'only I was being released'; Japanese 解放されるのは私だけ means 'only I was being released', a different framing (focus on self vs. focus on Satoshi)

TextENGNew: ...With no one to vouch for him, would Satoshi-kun be subjected to even more questioning?
changeReason: Different: 'because he had nobody to pick him up' vs 'with no one to vouch for him'; Japanese 身元引受人のない means 'without a guarantor/person to vouch'

TextENGNew: ...But perhaps I should have been worrying more about myself.
Having boldly identified myself as Shion in front of the police, the main house must already know.
changeReason: Different: 'the hag had probably heard about me by now' vs 'Having boldly identified myself as Shion, the main house must already know'; Japanese 園崎本家にはバレている means 'the Sonozaki main house knows'; original says 'hag' while new says 'main house'

TextENGNew: With such a brazen public appearance, there was no talking my way out of it.
changeReason: Different: 'I couldn't hide any longer' vs 'there was no talking my way out of it'; Japanese 言い逃れはできない means 'cannot talk one's way out', more specific than just 'couldn't hide'

TextENGNew: I would be hauled before my grandmother and subjected to some form of punishment.
changeReason: New translation adds detail: 'hauled before my grandmother and subjected to some form of punishment' vs 'receive some sort of punishment'; Japanese 祖母の前に引っ立てられ means 'dragged before grandmother'

TextENGNew: What form that punishment would take, I honestly couldn't imagine.
A protector stepping in, someone turning a blind eye, everything blowing over -- that sort of thing simply didn't happen in our world.
changeReason: Completely different: 'The degree of punishment for a traitor is always severe' vs 'A protector stepping in, someone turning a blind eye, everything blowing over -- that sort of thing simply didn't happen'; Japanese describes the impossibility of leniency, not severity of punishment

TextENGNew: Reputation. Appearances.
changeReason: Completely different: 'The punishment must be carried out' vs 'Reputation. Appearances.'; Japanese 面子とか体面とか means 'reputation and appearances', not about punishment execution

TextENGNew: ...Even if the person in question didn't want it, punishment had to be carried out as a gesture to the outside world.
changeReason: Different: 'If it's to protect the family's reputation, it's carried out no matter what' vs 'Even if the person didn't want it, punishment had to be carried out as a gesture to the outside world'; Japanese 対外的なジェスチャー means 'gesture to the outside', different motivation

TextENGNew: ...Maybe I should kiss my pinky finger goodbye while I still could.
I said something to the young detective escorting me out.
changeReason: Different: 'asked the young cop just in case' vs 'said something to the young detective escorting me out'; Japanese 言ってやる means 'told/said to him', not 'asked just in case'

TextENGNew: ``Hey, you know what?@k@vS06/06/150600909. In America, when a death row inmate gets caught in a state without the death penalty, they don't get extradited. You've heard of that, right?``
...Perhaps the reference was too obscure.@kThe detective looked utterly lost.
A jet-black car was waiting in front of the police station.
Several rough-looking men from my father's organization bowed deeply in greeting.
changeReason: New translation adds 人相のよろしくない (rough-looking/unsavory appearance) and 深々とお辞儀 (bowed deeply), details from the Japanese absent in the original

TextENGNew: ``It's been a while, Shion-san.``
``...Where's Kasai?``
Kasai had been my most devoted ally, arranging everything from my escape from the academy to my hideout in Okinomiya.@k...He was almost certainly not unscathed.
changeReason: New translation expands significantly: 'helping me ever since my escape' vs 'my most devoted ally, arranging everything from my escape to my hideout in Okinomiya'; Japanese provides more detail about Kasai's role

TextENGNew: ``...How's Sis doing?@k@vS06/06/150600912. Did she say anything?``
``...We were only instructed to bring you to the main house.@k@vS06/00/bukaA15004. Please direct any questions there.``
He told me curtly, then gestured for me to get in the car.
changeReason: Different: 'After saying that, he escorted me into the car' vs 'He told me curtly, then gestured for me to get in'; Japanese 素っ気なく means 'curtly/bluntly', absent from original

TextENGNew: One man on either side of me.@k...They clearly had no intention of letting me escape.
...Thinking of Satoshi-kun, still trapped inside the station, I looked back at the Okinomiya precinct.
changeReason: New translation adds 'still trapped inside the station' and 'Okinomiya precinct' (興宮署), details from the Japanese

TextENGNew: Hang in there, Satoshi-kun...@k...I'll grin and bear it too.
The car lurched into motion without ceremony.
I couldn't recall a single happy occasion that had brought me to the Sonozaki main house.
changeReason: Different: 'It was never a good occasion' vs 'I couldn't recall a single happy occasion'; Japanese ろくな時に招かれた記憶がない means 'no memory of being invited for a decent occasion'

TextENGNew: Except for when we were truly little, back when my sister and I were still too young to understand what the Sonozaki name meant, and could still play freely together.
...Every visit since had been for some stomach-churning family council or the like...
changeReason: Different: 'I'd been here only when I had to attend boring family councils' vs 'Every visit since had been for some stomach-churning family council'; Japanese 胃が痛くなるような means 'stomach-ache inducing', not 'boring'

TextENGNew: The car stopped not at the front gate but around the back.
changeReason: New translation adds 'not at the front gate but' (表門でなく), specifying the contrast between front and back entrances present in Japanese

TextENGNew: Only those with nothing to be ashamed of enter through the front.
changeReason: Different: 'Traitors aren't allowed in through the main gate' vs 'Only those with nothing to be ashamed of enter through the front'; Japanese 後ろめたくない人間 means 'people with nothing to be ashamed of', not 'non-traitors'

TextENGNew: When I stepped out, Sis was the only one there to meet me.
I started to call out casually, but the coldness in her eyes made me swallow the words.
``...It's been a while, Shion.@k@vS06/03/150300186. I didn't expect to see you outside of New Year's.``
The stiff formality in her voice sent something cold crawling up my spine.
``Mion-sama, how lovely to see you as well.@k@vS06/06/150600914. Being graced with your presence outside of New Year's makes this a three-cent blessing indeed.``
I returned fire with every ounce of sarcasm I could muster, matching her frosty tone.
changeReason: Different: 'I responded, trying to sound likewise' vs 'I returned fire with every ounce of sarcasm I could muster'; Japanese 精一杯の嫌味を込めて means 'packed with maximum sarcasm'

TextENGNew: ``The head of the family is deeply displeased.@k@vS06/03/150300188. I look forward to hearing how you plan to explain yourself.``
changeReason: Different: 'Our leader' vs 'The head of the family'; Japanese 頭首 means 'family head'. Also 'I wonder' vs 'I look forward to hearing'

TextENGNew: ``...I don't have anything to explain.``
There was nothing to explain.
I couldn't stand the academy, so I left.
That was all there was to it.
``Shion, come with me.@k@vS06/03/150300190. Everyone is waiting.``
Led by Mion, we began walking...
Not toward the house. Toward the garden.
...And when we headed deep into the vast garden, toward the forest at its far edge, I realized where they were taking me.
These woods at the back were the very place we'd been strictly forbidden from approaching when we were children, back when we knew nothing.
changeReason: New translation adds context: 'the very place we'd been strictly forbidden from approaching when we were children, back when we knew nothing' vs 'we were told never to go near'; Japanese provides more detail about their ignorance as children

TextENGNew: ...What lay deep within... I'd heard through vague rumors.
There was supposedly a hidden underground entrance, leading to rooms used for punishing those who crossed the Sonozaki family.
changeReason: New translation adds '秘密の地下の入口' (secret underground entrance), specifying 'underground' from the Japanese

TextENGNew: The more you learned about the black rumors surrounding the main house, the more credible those stories became.
...And yet, some part of me had always remained half-skeptical.
I'd never dreamed I would learn the truth with my own body...
changeReason: Different: 'see the torture room for myself' vs 'learn the truth with my own body'; Japanese 自らの身をもって知れる means 'learn through one's own body/experience', implying she'll be tortured, not just see it

TextENGNew: We eventually came to a place deep in the dense forest where the ground opened into a wide basin.
changeReason: Different: 'a concave clearing' vs 'a place where the ground opened into a wide basin'; Japanese 大きく盆地になっている means 'formed a large basin', more specific

TextENGNew: At the bottom of that bowl, I could see a steel door reminiscent of a bomb shelter.
``...So this is the rumored underground laboratory?``
I was startled to hear my own voice.
...It was trembling.@k...I was more frightened than I'd realized.
No one responded to my question, Mion included.@k...Only the cutting silence pressed against me like a blade.
changeReason: New translation adds '魅音も含め' (Mion included) and 'pressed against me like a blade' for 張りつめた空気が痛い (the taut atmosphere was painful), both from the Japanese

TextENGNew: ...Twins are an ill omen.@k...So decreed the Sonozaki family code.
changeReason: Completely different: 'one of the twins must be eliminated' vs 'Twins are an ill omen. So decreed the Sonozaki family code'; Japanese 双子は不吉の象徴 means 'twins are symbols of ill omen', not about elimination directly

TextENGNew: Then was I... could I actually be... eliminated?
I realized that my assumption I'd get off with just a finger or so was not resolve -- it had been nothing but naive wishful thinking...
changeReason: Significantly different: 'I had been underestimating my punishment' vs 'my assumption I'd get off with just a finger was not resolve -- it had been nothing but naive wishful thinking'; Japanese specifically mentions 指の一本 (one finger) and 甘え (naivete/dependence)

TextENGNew: I'd always said the Sonozaki main house was terrifying, and intellectually I'd understood that. But I'd never truly grasped it.
changeReason: Different: 'I hadn't been aware of the real terror until now' vs 'I'd always said the Sonozaki main house was terrifying, and intellectually I'd understood that. But I'd never truly grasped it'; Japanese distinguishes between 言葉の上では (in words) and 本当の意味では (true meaning)

TextENGNew: Because... the air beyond that steel door was something I'd never smelled before -- an air of dread so thick it was suffocating, and yet unmistakably, inescapably real.
changeReason: New translation significantly expands the sensory description of the underground atmosphere, capturing the Japanese nuance of dread and reality

TextENGNew: ...The moment I acknowledged my own fear, it was over.
I wanted to spit out defiant words, to kindle some false bravado inside me, but my lips had gone bone-dry without my noticing.@k...I could feel my fingertips fidgeting, restless and uncontrolled.
Down a narrow corridor, descending several flights of stairs.
Through another massive steel door.
...I had arrived at that room.
It was... a spacious room.@k...And the most bizarre room I had ever seen.
The room was split into two halves, each with a completely different character.

One half was a traditional Japanese sitting room.@k...There, occupying the seats of honor as in a family council, sat over a dozen relatives, all wearing expressions of eerie blankness on their cushions.
The other half had tiled floors and walls, like some enormous communal shower room.
This hybrid space -- formal sitting room fused with bathhouse -- was already thoroughly abnormal.
...And then... no, 'and then' is the wrong way to put it.
Because these were the very first things my eyes had seized upon when I entered.
Before the sitting room, before the tiles -- far, far before any of that.@kAnd yet... the very last things I wanted to acknowledge.
...My legs turned to straw.@kMy knees nearly buckled.
But they wouldn't even let me collapse.
From the moment I entered this room, two young men had locked my arms firmly in their grip.
Mounted on the shower-room walls were objects I'd never seen before -- yet whose purpose I could guess with horrible clarity.
...Ahh, calling them 'objects' was itself a cowardly evasion.
I should have known perfectly well what to call them.
...Ahhhh... I knew. I accepted it.
I understood that they were torture instruments... grotesquely warped into shapes that seemed to belong to another world entirely...
``Damned fool.@k@vS06/17/152300015. What face did you think you could show, crawling back here...``
The grandmother, seated at the front of the room, spoke in a voice so terrifying it couldn't help but cow every listener... slowly, as if letting each word soak in.
After that she ranted at length about something dreadful, but her accent was so thick with fury that I could barely make out the words.
...But even without catching every word... I could understand what she was trying to say.
...The grandmother had been prepared to overlook my escape from the academy.
But the moment I'd identified myself as Sonozaki Shion to the police, that option vanished.
With it now publicly known that I'd defied the family head's orders, the Sonozaki main house had no choice but to punish me.
...And above all else.
What the grandmother found most intolerable.
...Was that I had shielded Satoshi-kun.
...The Houjou family had been the ringleaders of the faction that defied the Three Families' wishes during the dam war.
The enmity between them and the Sonozaki family that had persisted since then extended even to Satoshi-kun.
That I, a Sonozaki, had any connection to Houjou Satoshi was unacceptable to her.
That was... deeply unexpected to me.
I had escaped from the academy that the grandmother had placed me in.
I'd assumed that was what had earned her wrath.
...But in reality, what truly displeased her was the pairing of me and Satoshi-kun.
I was filled with disbelief.
...I knew full well that during the dam war, Satoshi-kun's parents had served as standard-bearers for the pro-dam faction, obstructing the village's united front.
But I'd believed that was his parents' affair, not something their son should be held accountable for.
``...But that has nothing to do with Satoshi-kun...@k@vS06/06/150600918. Even if his parents deserved it, Satoshi-kun personally bears no responsi--``
``SILENCE!!!!``
The grandmother's thundering shout cut me off before I could finish, and I cringed involuntarily.
She spewed vile abuse at the Houjou family's transgressions and declared that Satoshi-kun was equally the spawn of those who'd defied the Three Families.
...As I listened, fury began to build inside me.
What had Satoshi-kun ever done?@kWhat crime could possibly be laid at his feet?
By the time I realized it, I'd already spoken aloud the very thoughts in my heart.
Seeing the grandmother's eyes blaze with mounting rage, I finally understood what I'd just said.
And so I resolved to say it again -- this time deliberately, of my own will.
``...What are you even saying, you old hag?@k@vS06/06/150600920. I've been sitting here listening to your drivel and it's all garbage!``
``Huhh?!@k@vS06/17/152300018. How dare you speak to me that way!!``
``Shut the hell up! Let me finish, you old fool!!!!``
I bellowed loud enough to drown out even the grandmother's roar.
``What do you even know about Satoshi-kun?!@k@vS06/06/150600923. You don't know the first thing about him, and yet you dismiss him like he's vermin!!!``
``I know exactly how wonderful a person Satoshi-kun is!@k@vS06/06/150600925. Is the problem that he's a Houjou?@k@vS06/06/150600926. How ridiculous!!@k@vS06/06/150600927. How absolutely pathetic!!``
``Is it really that unbearable that a Sonozaki was with a Houjou?!@k@vS06/06/150600929. What petty, small-minded, idiotic garbage!!!``
``I never thought I'd get to live out my own bargain-bin Romeo and Juliet!@k@vS06/06/150600931. Ah ha ha ha ha ha!!!``
No matter how boldly I forced the words out, my voice was cracking.
Tears were streaming down my face, leaving it a mess.@kBut... I had no intention of stopping this outpouring.
...Even if this righteous fury was nothing more than a temporary reprieve from fear.
The grandmother, living up to her title in every sense, was glaring at me with heaving breaths.
I glared right back, panting just as hard, trying to devour her with my eyes alone.
...Eventually.@kI realized the grandmother's fixation was specifically on my relationship with Satoshi-kun.
...She wasn't merely condemning us for being together. It went deeper than that.
``...I see... so Mion told you about that too...``
Mion's face was as expressionless as a Noh mask.@kShe didn't flinch under my venomous gaze.
...What the grandmother truly found intolerable.
...Was that I had romantic feelings for Satoshi-kun...
``Ahahahahahahaha!@k@vS06/06/150600934. Aha hahahahahahaha!!``

``It's true -- I couldn't care less about being a Sonozaki, or about Satoshi-kun being a Houjou, or about family honor, or public appearances!@k@vS06/06/150600936. Yes, I'll admit it! I'll admit everything!``
``I love Houjou Satoshi-kun.@k@vS06/06/150600938. I love him with all my heart!@k@vS06/06/150600939. Is that so wrong?!@k@vS06/06/150600940. Is loving someone really such a terrible sin?!``
Behind the grandmother, I could see relatives shaking their heads at the irreversible turn things had taken, or looking down in dismay.
Among them... I spotted my mother, who had surely fought hardest to protect me.
She was no longer looking at me.
All she could do was maintain her silence and stare at the tatami.
``Can you all hear me back there?!@k@vS06/06/150600942. Am I saying something crazy?!``

``If any of you bothered to get to know Satoshi-kun, you'd see in an instant what a wonderful person he is, and you'd understand why I fell for him!!@k@vS06/06/150600944. So why won't you even try to understand before you judge?!``
Mion raised her hand in a gesture that said 'that's enough,' silencing me.
Then she walked slowly toward me.
``...That will do, Shion.@k@vS06/03/150300192. I understand what you wanted to say, and the resolve behind it.``
Then Mion brought her face so close our foreheads nearly touched, and whispered in a voice only I could hear.

``...I understand your resolve, Shion.@k@vS06/03/150300194. ...But after going this far, no one can shield you anymore.@k@vS06/03/150300195. ...You need to show that you can settle accounts on your own.``
``...Settle accounts?!@k@vS06/06/150600947. Why should I?!@k@vS06/06/150600948. What I did wasn't--``
``Shion.``
Mion's face hardened back into the cold mask of the heir apparent... and she spoke as if admonishing me.

``...What you call individual reasoning is probably correct.@k@vS06/03/150300198. ...But you understand this, don't you?@k@vS06/03/150300199. This is Hinamizawa, and we are the Sonozaki family. Individual logic doesn't always prevail here.``
``And you are, like it or not, the twin sister of Sonozaki Mion, next head of the Sonozaki family.@k@vS06/03/150300202. ...And yet--``
I didn't let Mion finish. I cut her off with my own words.

``We've never seen eye to eye on this since I came back.@k@vS06/06/150600950. It's always 'grown-up reasons' this and 'grown-up reasons' that!@k@vS06/06/150600951. Hinamizawa? The Sonozaki family? So what?!``
``Listen!``
Mion interrupted me in turn, just as I had done to her.
``...Shion, in the course of establishing your life in Okinomiya... how many people did you depend on?``
...Something cold slithered up my spine.
``...Kasai-san is in the cell at the back.``
``Wh-Why is Kasai there?!``
It shouldn't have surprised me.
...The moment I was caught, everyone who'd sheltered me shared the same fate.@k...Their crime of defying the family head was identical to mine.
I... might actually be getting off easy.
...Kasai, locked in that back cell, and Uncle Yoshiroh, who'd surely be dragged here soon --@k...they were probably being treated far more roughly.
Everyone who'd indulged my selfishness... was now paying for it.
``...Even if you're prepared for whatever happens to you, Shion.@k@vS06/03/150300205. Have you spared a thought for what happens to Kasai-san and Uncle Yoshiroh, who helped you?``
The scalding sensation that had filled my head was rapidly draining away.@k...My defiance had long since been spent.
Whatever torment they inflicted on me alone, I could surely endure.
But what I couldn't stomach was causing harm to the people who'd defied the main house for my sake.

``...Shion.@k@vS06/03/150300207. Apologize to Granny.@k@vS06/03/150300208. If you demonstrate that you can settle your own accounts, this can end with you alone.@k@vS06/03/150300209. ...The others won't be touched.``
``But... but, Mion...@k@vS06/06/150600958. Did I really say something wrong...?@k@vS06/06/150600959. Was what I did... really so terrible...?``
...Seeing that I lacked the resolve to settle accounts, Mion retracted the brief flash of compassion she'd shown... and her expression returned to its former coldness.
She turned her back on me and walked toward the sitting room.
...I had picked a fight with the grandmother, full of bluster.
...I had insisted I'd done nothing wrong.
...But I'd dragged everyone who'd helped me into this mess.
...That was my responsibility, without anyone needing to point it out.
...A sin that I alone should bear... and they bore no fault whatsoever.
...Yes, just as Satoshi-kun himself was blameless, so too were Kasai and Uncle Yoshiroh.
At that moment... as if she'd read every thought in my heart... Mion turned back and... gave the smallest nod.
Just like Kasai and Uncle Yoshiroh.
...Even blameless Satoshi-kun... might be dragged into this.
No... that absolutely could not happen...@kNot Satoshi-kun -- no, I mean -- none of them deserve this.@kIf I alone can settle this, then please, spare them all...!
``...What is it, Shion...?``
Tears traced countless paths down my crumpled face.
...My cheap pride, my petty vanity -- none of it mattered anymore.
``...I'm sorry...@k@vS06/06/150600963. I was wrong...@k@vS06/06/150600964. ...Please forgive me, Honored Leader.``
That should have been what the grandmother wanted to hear.@k...And yet the old hag's face remained displeased.
...Of course it did.
...I had berated her in front of the entire assembled family.@k...There was no way she could simply let me off after that.
In the grandmother's stead, it was Mion, the next head, who spoke.
``Then, Shion.@k@vS06/03/150300212. ...How do you intend to settle your accounts?``
``S-Settle...? What do you want me to...?``
Being told to 'settle accounts' while surrounded by those dreadful torture instruments on the walls -- nothing could be more terrifying.
...I could only tremble anew at the sheer horror of those words.
As I stood paralyzed with fear, Mion took pity and whispered something to the grandmother.
...The grandmother nodded.
Mion called over some young men and gave them instructions.
They went to the wall and took down one of the menacing instruments...
...then brought a table before me and placed the device on it.
``...What is... this...?``
Trembling, I looked at the sinister tool on the table.
At first glance, it resembled an enormously oversized nail clipper.
``One fingernail for each debt you've incurred.@k@vS06/03/150300216. ...Sonozaki Shion.@k@vS06/03/150300217. You may choose any fingers you wish.@k@vS06/03/150300218. Three fingernails.``
Three.@k...One for Kasai.@kOne for Uncle Yoshiroh.@k...And one for... Satoshi-kun.
...The number was more than fair.
``Will you... really spare the others... if I do this...?``
``Promise me...@k@vS06/06/150600970. Promise that if I tear off my own nails... you'll forgive everyone else...``
``This is your atonement, not a negotiation.@k@vS06/03/150300221. ...If this method doesn't suit you--``
``I'll do it, so wait!!!@k@vS06/06/150600972. Just wait... I'm doing it... I'll do it...``
I placed my trembling left hand against the instrument's ghastly beak...
Immediately, the young men locked my hand and the device together with a separate restraining apparatus.
Each step of this process@kdrove home the reality of what was about to happen,@k...and closed off every avenue of escape.
Even now, somewhere in my heart, I'd been clinging to the hope that they might let it end as just a gesture.
I thought I'd already thrown away such naivety...
But as they tightened the noose bit by bit, the wishful thinking I'd believed gone kept being squeezed from my chest... spilling out as tears.
Once they'd confirmed my hand was firmly secured, a cold, agonizing silence settled in -- the kind that demanded action.
``...So... wh-what... do I do now...?``
One of the young men pointed to the handle of this monstrous nail-clipper and told me to either squeeze it or strike it.
After that, no one forced anything.
An aching silence in which the only sound was my sweat dripping onto the surface.
The witnesses watched in silence, waiting to see me carry out my atonement.
...The grandmother, and Mion too.@k...They waited, unblinking, for me to settle my own debt with my own hands.
...Continuing to tremble and refuse was surely one option.
...But if I chose that, it wouldn't count as settling accounts.
Settling accounts meant: my punishment alone would atone for the sin.
...If I refused, the punishment would extend to every person close to me who had helped.
``Hah... hah... hahhh... hahhh...``
I knew.@kIf I tore off three of my own fingernails, no one else would be harmed.
This was, if anything, an extraordinarily lenient deal.
...It was only three nails because it was me.
...If it were Kasai, they wouldn't stop at nails -- it would be fingers, or something far worse.
I knew. I knew that.
Escaping the academy had been my selfish whim.
What crime had Kasai committed, other than gladly helping me?
I knew. I knew that.
What crime had Uncle Yoshiroh committed, other than finding me work and helping me make ends meet?
I now understood all too well that the grandmother and the relatives still despised the Houjou name...
So if I failed to settle accounts here... I couldn't imagine what horrors they'd visit on Satoshi-kun...
Would they... drag Satoshi-kun to this very room? Place this same instrument before him?
...Would they tell him to tear off his own nails in exchange for forgiving me? Would they force him to do the same...?
...Satoshi-kun would do it.
...If sacrificing himself could save someone, Satoshi-kun would do it without a moment's hesitation.
Sweat beaded across my entire face, channeling along the ridge of my nose, gathering into droplets at its tip that fell steadily onto the table.
...Do it... just do it.
...It's only three fingernails.
...I'm getting off this lightly only because it's me.
If I refuse... everyone else will pay in far worse ways...
I can't let Kasai or Uncle Yoshiroh or... Satoshi-kun suffer because of me...
I alone must bear this responsibility...
So... so... I just have to endure the pain that's about to come...
That's right... do it all at once.
That way it hurts less, that way it's less terrifying...!
I held my final breath.
Don't be afraid.
...I can do this. I can endure this...!
``AAAAAHHHHHHHH!!``
I brought my raised fist crashing down onto the handle of the torture device.
In that instant, a pain unlike anything I had ever experienced in my life attacked from an angle I didn't know existed.
I clenched my teeth with all my might.
Hard enough to crack my molars!
I had to do this... two more times?
I looked at the grandmother and Mion, begging with my eyes for mercy.
There wasn't a trace of sympathy to be found.
...They had no intention of relenting.
Three nails meant three nails. Absolutely and without exception.
They wouldn't forgive me until I endured this agony two more times and tore off two more nails...!
``...Mion... please... isn't this enough...?``
``...It really... hurts... heh... heh heh heh...``
``...Shion.@k@vS06/03/150300226. ...Just a little more. You can do it.``
``No no no no!!@k@vS06/06/150601001. I really can't! It really hurts! It hurts so much!!``
Mion shook her head slightly, then signaled the young men with her chin.
...There was no hesitation.
If... there was anything to be grateful for.
...It was that the one who carried it out was experienced, and cleanly removed both remaining nails in two swift strokes.
Amid the pain and the screaming... as I went on shrieking, my consciousness grew thinner and thinner, and fainter and fainter...
